critiques.summary: I reviewed the Set 093 spec/change-log/disposition plus the shipped implementation across the module-action protocol, host dispatch, webview client/CSS, authoring seams, assign-legacy writer, command registration, and the added unit/Playwright coverage. The load-bearing claims around row-strip/context/palette parity, click-path QuickPick retirement, loud stale-slug failure, fallback suppression, pseudo-row repo-level targeting, atomic format-preserving module stamping, and the Open Plan palette mirror all match the code I opened, and the tests exercise the key regressions called out in the docs.

VERIFIED. I have reviewed the implementation of the new Work Explorer module-row interaction surface. The client-side UI changes in `media/session-sets-tree/client.js` correctly render the new action strip and context menu, and post the new `moduleAction` and `showModuleContextMenu` messages as described. The webview protocol in `types/sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts` is updated with the correct message shapes. The host-side handling in `providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts` correctly uses the pure, strict narrowing functions from `utils/moduleActionNarrowing.ts` to validate all incoming messages, dropping malformed or out-of-bounds requests as designed. This critical security and correctness boundary is thoroughly tested in `test/suite/moduleActionNarrowing.test.ts`, which verifies all claimed invariants. The change successfully retires the module-selection QuickPick for these user flows by correctly threading the `preselectedSlug` to the underlying authoring commands. The implementation is robust, well-tested, and consistent with all load-bearing claims.
